title: Dumbell Squat Press is the BEST weight exersize. EVERYBODY should do it on YouTube

enrichment.summary: Kyle introduces an exercise he invented called the 'dumbbell squat to overhead press ladder,' describing the movement, rep scheme, and a progressive weight structure starting at 10 lbs and going up to 45 lbs. He explains why he thinks it's the best full-body dumbbell exercise and says his next video will be his first filmed attempt.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: believes it is the best full-body dumbbell exercise everyone should try
recommends filming with a laptop on a chair to capture full-body movement
advocates starting light to calibrate form and avoid injury with heavier weights
candidly admits he is fat at ~32% body fat and expects to struggle around 25–35 lbs

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle's best exercise on the planet is called the dumbbell ladder, specifically the dumbbell squat to overhead press ladder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The dumbbell ladder is the perfect exercise because it works out every part of the body.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The dumbbell ladder involves picking up a dumbbell, holding it like a suitcase, squatting deeply until elbows touch knees, standing up, and pressing the dumbbell overhead.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The dumbbell ladder is done in three sets of ten reps, with ten reps in a minute, followed by a 30-second wait before the next two sets.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: After completing the sets, there is a two-minute wait before moving to a higher weight.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle starts the dumbbell ladder with ten-pound weights and progresses through fifteen, twenty-five, thirty-five, and forty-five pounds.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle came up with the dumbbell ladder idea with the help of ChatGPT and even asked it how much it thought people could achieve.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The dumbbell ladder works the back, thighs, quads, shoulders, triceps, and biceps, and tests flexibility, agility, and balance.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is not flexible enough to do the dumbbell ladder with his feet pointing straight and close together, so he uses a sumo squat stance.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes everyone should try the dumbbell ladder because it demonstrates strength and physique.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Filming the dumbbell ladder involves placing a laptop on a chair to capture the whole body and movement.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle suggests doing sets of the dumbbell ladder while turning around to show the body from different angles.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: When Kyle saw himself in a sample video of the dumbbell ladder, he noticed he was fat, with a body fat percentage of around 32%.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks pull-ups could be another good exercise but prefers the dumbbell ladder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle can do pull-ups with his feet on a chair, aiming for three sets of ten.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle predicts he will start the dumbbell ladder with a low weight to calibrate his body for the movement.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes starting with a low weight prevents lifting too heavy and getting into trouble.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks he is super weak and might struggle with the twenty-five-pound dumbbells, possibly not attempting the thirty-five-pound ones.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: ChatGPT predicted Kyle could handle the thirty-five-pound dumbbells based on a photo of his younger, lighter self at 150 pounds.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle's heaviest dumbbells are forty-five pounds, and he plans to do two sets with them before descending back through the weights.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle suggests repeating the dumbbell ladder a few times to ensure good form and possibly turning it into a race.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle concludes that the dumbbell ladder is the best exercise and wishes everyone good luck trying it.

transcript.text: Let me tell you about my new idea, my best exercise on the planet called the dumbbell ladder.
Or the, if you want to be more specific, dumbbell squat to overhead press ladder that I want to do like every day.
It's the perfect exercise because it works out every part of you.
So, what it is, is you pick up the dumbbell and then you hold it down like this, you know, like you hold a suitcase.
And then you sit down in a squat and your elbows touch your knees.
So, you're in a deep squat, deep enough to do that.
And then you stand up again like this and then you do the dumbbell like that and then you put it down and then you do another one.
So, you do three sets of ten.
It's done by reps.
And like maybe ten sets or ten reps in a minute.
And then wait 30 seconds and then you do the other two.
And then you wait two minutes and then you go up to the higher weight.
So, you start at ten.
I have weights ten, fifteen, twenty-five, thirty-five, forty-five.
So, I'm going to see if I can get how far I can get and then train for it.
I came up with the idea kind of with ChatGTP and even asked it how much it thought people could get and stuff.
But, yeah, so, it's the best exercise because it does every part of your body, you know.
Your back, big time.
Thighs, quads, big time.
And then your shoulders and your triceps.
Yeah, and even biceps as you lift it up.
So, and it tests your flexibility and your agility and balance.
I'm not flexible to do it with both of my feet to like pointing straight close together.
Because when I go all the way down for my elbows to touch my knees, my heels come up.
So, I have to put my legs like sumo squat kind of farther apart than the shoulders and pointing out a little bit.
But if I do that, I can touch my elbows to my knees.
So, I'm going to do it like that.
And, yeah, I think that everybody should try to do it.
I think it's the best dumbbell exercise to demonstrate your strength but also your physique.
Because the way that you film it is just you just put your laptop on a chair in front of you.
And so, it can see.
It's so close.
It sees your whole body.
And it sees your whole movement.
It doesn't see the ground, but it sees your elbows touch your knees and then your elbows.
So, showing that you're straight up.
And, which is all you need.
And then you can do sets turning around.
So, the first one facing and then the next one facing left and right and then even back.
So, you see the whole body in all these different positions.
And, yeah, like when I saw myself, when I did the sample, I was fat.
But, I'm at like 32%.
So, yeah, I think everybody should do it.
Then you know how strong people are.
Maybe there's another exercise you could do that's more pulling, like pull-ups.
But, I have a pull-up bar.
I can do those with my feet on the chair.
But, like three sets of ten.
I'm going to try that pretty soon.
So, anyways, yeah, that's my demonstration video.
And, so, the next video will be my first attempt.
I predict I start on a low weight because it's good to calibrate the body for the movement, you know.
So, you're ready by the time the heavier ones come.
Yeah.
And, maybe also so you're a little more fatigued by the time they come.
So, you don't get yourself in trouble trying to lift too heavy a weight.
But, oh, man, I'm super weak.
I wouldn't be surprised if I struggle through the 25 and I only make it through that.
And, I don't try 35.
Or, maybe I can do one or two or 35.
Yeah, and I think that's what chat TTP.
Actually, I need to upload a photo of myself.
That's what it predicted I could do when I uploaded a photo of my younger, lighter self.
Like 150 pounds.
But, and then, so, I have 45s as the heaviest.
So, then you could try to do the latter, like, and back.
So, then you do two sets of 45.
And, then you go back down.
So, then it's 35, 25, 15, and 10.
And, then that's the whole thing.
And, then, after that, you do it a few times.
That's so that your form is good.
Maybe you can turn that into, like, a race or something.
So, anyway.
That's the exercise.
I think it's the best one and everybody should do it.
Good luck.
